records.ety: [OF. carpite rug, soft of cloth, F. carpette coarse packing cloth, rug (cf. It. carpita rug, blanket), LL. carpeta, carpita, woolly cloths, fr. L. carpere to pluck, to card (wool); cf. Gr. karpo`s fruit, E. Harvest.]

defs: A heavy woven or felted fabric, usually of wool, but also of cotton, hemp, straw, etc.; esp. a floor covering made in breadths to be sewed together and nailed to the floor, as distinguished from a rug or mat; originally, also, a wrought cover for tables.
quotes: Tables and beds covered with copes instead of carpets and coverlets.

defs: A smooth soft covering resembling or suggesting a carpet.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Carpeted; p. pr. & vb. n. Carpeting.]

defs: To cover with, or as with, a carpet; to spread with carpets; to furnish with a carpet or carpets.
